#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
fi
APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    cd ../../
    if [ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 0 ]; then
        build.sh "NX45" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    else
        build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    fi
    build_ret=$?
    cd ${CWD}

    if [ ${build_ret} -ne 0 ]; then
        exit 1
    fi
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."
    echo "    Each check-sum MUST be identical with X86."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_pnet -f Makefile_test.mak
    make pnet -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -w"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} pnet.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "pnet"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean_mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak
        make mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} mtcnn.adx
    fi

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "mtcnn"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN_DW...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH
        make -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_test"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_NONSQ_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_nonsq_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_nonsq_test"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC} -eq 1 ]];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running unit func...."
    echo "    Checking accuracy and collect the cycle for unit functions."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
fi
APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    cd ../../
    if [ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 0 ]; then
        build.sh "NX45" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    else
        build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    fi
    build_ret=$?
    cd ${CWD}

    if [ ${build_ret} -ne 0 ]; then
        exit 1
    fi
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."
    echo "    Each check-sum MUST be identical with X86."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_pnet -f Makefile_test.mak
    make pnet -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -w"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} pnet.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "pnet"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean_mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak
        make mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} mtcnn.adx
    fi

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "mtcnn"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN_DW...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH
        make -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_test"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_NONSQ_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_nonsq_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_nonsq_test"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC} -eq 1 ]];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running unit func...."
    echo "    Checking accuracy and collect the cycle for unit functions."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

name: Build and Test Environment for libnn
description: Andes toolchain, QEMU paths, and run commands for building/testing libnn on AX45MPV with RVV
type: reference
---

## Andes Toolchain
- Path: `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/`
- Compiler: `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/riscv64-elf-gcc` (Andes v5d, GCC 14.2.0)
- Must be first on PATH to override system riscv64-elf-gcc (which lacks Andes extensions like `-mext-vector`)

## QEMU (Andes fork)
- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- Env var: `export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- Supports all Andes CPU models (ax45mpv, nx27v, ax66, etc.) and `andes_ae350` machine

## Running Unit Tests for AX45MPV

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/build-ast540/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
bash ./run.sh
```

- Default wrapper: `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` (VLEN=512)

- CPU model used: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={512|1024}`

## User's Focus
- Primary target: AX45MPV with VLEN=1024 (`v1024_d1024_b512`)
- 294/294 unit tests pass at 100% (verified 2026-03-20)


file.content:
---
name: optimize-libnn
description: RVV optimization knowledge skill for libnn neural network library functions
user_invocable: true
---

# RVV Optimization Skill for libnn

You are an expert RISC-V Vector Extension (RVV) optimization engineer working on the Andes libnn neural network library. Your task is to optimize C functions using RVV intrinsics to match or exceed expert-written performance.

## Step 1: Analyze the Target Function

Before writing any code, read and understand:

1. **The plain-C fallback** (the `#else` branch) — understand the algorithm
2. **The function signature** — input/output types, quantization parameters
3. **The existing DSP path** (if any) — shows what parallelism experts already exploit
4. **Similar functions that already have RVV** — find patterns to reuse

Classify the function into one of these categories:

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→# Input
     4→# $1: toolcahin prefix
     5→# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
     6→# $3: extra flags
     7→################################################################################
     8→
     9→#######################################################
    10→# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
    11→#   $1: test function name
    12→#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
    13→#######################################################
    14→function run_and_check {
    15→    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    16→    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
    17→        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))
    18→
    19→        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    20→            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
    21→        fi
    22→        return 0
    23→    fi
    24→
    25→    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    26→    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
    27→        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))
    28→
    29→        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    30→            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
    31→        fi
    32→        return 0
    33→    fi
    34→
    35→    echo "$1"
    36→    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    37→    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    38→    set -x
    39→        ${build_cmd}
    40→        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
    41→        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
    42→            # dump the disassembly
    43→            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"
    44→
    45→            echo "Start at $(date)"
    46→            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
    47→            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
    48→            echo "End at $(date)"
    49→        fi
    50→    set +x
    51→
    52→    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
    53→        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
    54→        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
    55→        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
    56→        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
    57→        # the test program is executed.
    58→        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
    59→        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    60→            fail_list+=("${1}")
    61→            echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log}
    62→        else
    63→            pass_num=$((pass_num + 1))
    64→        fi
    65→
    66→        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    67→            #dump inst/cycle into csv
    68→            echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${CSV_OUT}
    69→            grep -w "inst" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
    70→            grep -w "cycle count" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
    71→            #grep -w "miss stall" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $7}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
    72→            echo >> ${CSV_OUT}  #new line
    73→        fi
    74→    else
    75→        fail_list+=("${1}")
    76→    fi
    77→}

    80→#                                   main                                       #
    81→################################################################################
    82→ENA_TEST_ALL=1
    83→ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=0
    84→ENA_BASIC_TEST=0
    85→ENA_CONCATE_TEST=0
    86→ENA_CONV_TEST=0
    87→ENA_FC_TEST=0
    88→ENA_POOLING_TEST=0
    89→ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST=0
    90→ENA_UTIL_TEST=0
    91→
    92→ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0           # only for the test programs with DYNAMIC allocation buffers
    93→ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0          # only run large test case to measure the performance (
    94→                            # run the program with DYNAMIC allocation buffers)
    95→ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # grep instruction and cycle counts into csv
    96→ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC=0   # run the test prgram with STATIC allocation buffers
    97→ENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0          # The testers can turn on this switch as running the
    98→                            # test programs in the environment not supporting
    99→                            # FILE I/O, e.g. m4 or m7. The inputs will be randomly
   100→                            # generated and the results will not be verified.
   101→                            # (this switch is ONLY for STATIC allocation buffer tests)
   102→ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER=0   # verify the wrapper header file between andes-nn and cmsis-nn
   103→
   104→# Since strided load is not supported now. If vector extension
   105→# is enabled, those functions use strided load won't be executed.
   106→# if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mvh"* ]]; then
   107→#     ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=1      #only run the test cases with unit stride acess
   108→# else
   109→    ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=0
   110→# fi
   111→
   112→#===== configure test environment_begin =====
   113→CC=${1}
   114→if [[ $CC =~ - ]]; then
   115→    OBJDUMP="${CC%-*}-objdump"
   116→else
   117→    OBJDUMP="objdump"
   118→fi
   119→SIM_WRAPPER=${2}
   120→EXTRA_FLAG="${3}"
   121→CFLAGS="-Wall -Werror -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
   122→MACRO="-DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=${ENA_RANDOM_INPUT}"
   123→UNIT_TEST_PATH=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
   124→UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../unit_performance"
   125→INC_DIR="-I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../Include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm-project/libhvm/include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include/"
   126→LINKED_LIB="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libnn.a"
   127→if [ ${ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC} -eq 1 ]; then
   128→    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/libnn.csv"
   129→    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/log"
   130→    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/adx"
   131→    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/objdump"
   132→    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}"
   133→    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_pf_"
   134→else    #ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC
   135→    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/libnn.csv"
   136→    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/log"
   137→    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/adx"
   138→    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/objdump"
   139→    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
   140→    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_"
   141→fi
   142→if [ ${ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER} -eq 1 ]; then
   143→    INC_DIR+=" -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../cmsis_include"
   144→    MACRO+=" -DENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER"
   145→fi
   146→if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
   147→    # As using the v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default for
   148→    # some floating-piont functions.
   149→    MACRO+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO"
   150→
   151→    # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
   152→    # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.
   153→    MACRO+=" -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
   154→fi
   155→if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
   156→    LINKED_LIB+=" ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm.a"
   157→fi
   158→#===== configure test environment_end =====
   159→
   160→# create folders and clean old files
   161→mkdir -p ${ADX_DIR}
   162→mkdir -p ${OBJDUMP_DIR}
   163→mkdir -p ${LOG_DIR}
   164→rm -f ${LOG_DIR}/*.log          # clean old logs
   165→rm -f ${ADX_DIR}/*.adx          # clean old executables
   166→rm -f ${OBJDUMP_DIR}/*.objdump  # clean old objdump files

   168→pass_num=0
   169→unsupport_num=0

   512→    "fc_u8_s16_s8_sym_bias_fast"
   513→    "fc_s8_s8_s8_sym_fast"
   514→    "fc_s8_s16_s8_sym_fast"
   515→    "fc_u8_u8_s8_sym_fast"
   516→    "fc_u8_s8_s8_sym_fast"
   517→    "fc_u8_s16_s8_sym_fast"
   518→    "fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast"
   519→    "fc_s16_s16_s16_sft_bias_fast"
   520→    "fc_mat_vec_s16_s16_s8_sft_bias_fast"
   521→)
   522→
   523→if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST} == 1 ]; then
   524→    test_list+=("${ACTIVATION[@]/%/:activation}")
   525→fi
   526→if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_BASIC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
   527→    test_list+=("${BASIC[@]/%/:basic}")
   528→fi
   529→if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONCATE_TEST} == 1 ]; then
   530→    test_list+=("${CONCATE[@]/%/:concatenation}")
   531→fi
   532→if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONV_TEST} == 1 ]; then
   533→    test_list+=("${CONV[@]/%/:convolution}")
   534→fi
   535→if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_FC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
   536→    test_list+=("${FC[@]/%/:fully_connected}")
   537→fi
   538→if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_POOLING_TEST} == 1 ]; then
   539→    test_list+=("${POOLING[@]/%/:pooling}")
   540→fi
   541→if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST} == 1 ]; then
   542→    test_list+=("${SOFTMAX[@]/%/:softmax}")
   543→fi
   544→if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_UTIL_TEST} == 1 ]; then
   545→    test_list+=("${UTIL[@]/%/:util}")
   546→fi
   547→
   548→#remove non-unit-stride test cases
   549→if [ ${ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY} == 1 ]; then
   550→    for FUN_NAME in "${NON_UNIT_STRIDE[@]}";
   551→    do
   552→        test_list=(${test_list[@]/$FUN_NAME})
   553→    done
   554→fi
   555→
   556→# record whether the zfh extension is enabled
   557→if [[ ${CFLAGS} == *"-mzfh"* ]]; then
   558→    ENA_FP16=1
   559→else
   560→    ENA_FP16=0
   561→fi
   562→
   563→# record whether the VL4 extension is enabled
   564→if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_VL4 ]]; then
   565→    ENA_VL4=1
   566→else
   567→    ENA_VL4=0
   568→fi
   569→
   570→if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
   571→    #init csv title
   572→    echo "group, function, inst, cycle" > ${CSV_OUT}
   573→fi
   574→
   575→#----- enable the LM switch -----
   576→if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
   577→    sed -i 's/CACHE="on"/CACHE="lm"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
   578→fi
   579→
   580→for item in "${test_list[@]}"; do
   581→    fun_name=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f1)
   582→    fun_group=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f2)
   583→    run_and_check "${fun_name}" "${fun_group}"
   584→done
   585→
   586→#----- restore the LM switch -----
   587→if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
   588→    sed -i 's/CACHE="lm"/CACHE="on"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
   589→fi
   590→
   591→# only show the execution summary as ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN is disabled
   592→if [ ${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -eq 0 ]; then
   593→    #show result summary
   594→    total_num=${#test_list[@]}
   595→    executed_num="$((total_num - unsupport_num))"
   596→    printf "\n========== accuracy checking summary ==========\n"
   597→    printf "Total functions:       %4d\n" "${#test_list[@]}"
   598→    printf "Executed functions:    %4d\n" "${executed_num}"
   599→    printf "Passed functions:      %4d (pass rate: %.2f%%)\n" "${pass_num}" "$(echo ${pass_num}*100/${executed_num} | bc -l)"
   600→    printf "Unsupported functions: %4d\n" "${unsupport_num}"
   601→fi
   602→
   603→# show fail list
   604→if [ ${#fail_list[@]} != 0 ]; then
   605→    echo
   606→    echo "failed funcion list:"
   607→    for FUN_NAME in "${fail_list[@]}"; do
   608→        echo ${FUN_NAME}
   609→    done
   610→fi
   611→

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input
# $1: toolcahin prefix
# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
# $3: extra flags
################################################################################

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$1"
    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    set -x
        ${build_cmd}
        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # dump the disassembly
            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"

            echo "Start at $(date)"
            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            echo "End at $(date)"
        fi
    set +x

    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
        # the test program is executed.
        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            fail_list+=("${1}")
            echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log}
        else
            pass_num=$((pass_num + 1))
        fi

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            #dump inst/cycle into csv
            echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "inst" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            grep -w "cycle count" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            #grep -w "miss stall" ${log} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $7}' >> ${CSV_OUT}
            echo >> ${CSV_OUT}  #new line
        fi
    else
        fail_list+=("${1}")
    fi
}

################################################################################
#                                   main                                       #
################################################################################
ENA_TEST_ALL=1
ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=0
ENA_BASIC_TEST=0
ENA_CONCATE_TEST=0
ENA_CONV_TEST=0
ENA_FC_TEST=0
ENA_POOLING_TEST=0
ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST=0
ENA_UTIL_TEST=0

ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0           # only for the test programs with DYNAMIC allocation buffers
ENA_MEASURE_PERF=0          # only run large test case to measure the performance (
                            # run the program with DYNAMIC allocation buffers)
ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV=0        # grep instruction and cycle counts into csv
ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC=0   # run the test prgram with STATIC allocation buffers
ENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0          # The testers can turn on this switch as running the
                            # test programs in the environment not supporting
                            # FILE I/O, e.g. m4 or m7. The inputs will be randomly
                            # generated and the results will not be verified.
                            # (this switch is ONLY for STATIC allocation buffer tests)
ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER=0   # verify the wrapper header file between andes-nn and cmsis-nn

# Since strided load is not supported now. If vector extension
# is enabled, those functions use strided load won't be executed.
# if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]] && [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mvh"* ]]; then
#     ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=1      #only run the test cases with unit stride acess
# else
    ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY=0
# fi

#===== configure test environment_begin =====
CC=${1}
if [[ $CC =~ - ]]; then
    OBJDUMP="${CC%-*}-objdump"
else
    OBJDUMP="objdump"
fi
SIM_WRAPPER=${2}
EXTRA_FLAG="${3}"
CFLAGS="-Wall -Werror -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
MACRO="-DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=${ENA_RANDOM_INPUT}"
UNIT_TEST_PATH=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../unit_performance"
INC_DIR="-I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../Include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm-project/libhvm/include/ -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include/"
LINKED_LIB="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libnn.a"
if [ ${ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC} -eq 1 ]; then
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_PERFORMANCE_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_pf_"
else    #ENA_MEASURE_PERF_STATIC
    CSV_OUT="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/libnn.csv"
    LOG_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/log"
    ADX_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/adx"
    OBJDUMP_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/objdump"
    TEST_SOURCE_DIR="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
    TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX="t_"
fi
if [ ${ENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER} -eq 1 ]; then
    INC_DIR+=" -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../cmsis_include"
    MACRO+=" -DENA_VERIFY_CMSIS_NN_WRAPPER"
fi
if [[ "${CFLAGS}" == *"-mext-vector"* ]]; then
    # As using the v-ext, now the fast algorithm will be applied by default for
    # some floating-piont functions.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO"

    # As measuring v-ext algorithm on cacheable environment, we can enable
    # ENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP switch to get better performance.
    MACRO+=" -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
fi
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
    LINKED_LIB+=" ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/../../libhvm.a"
fi
#===== configure test environment_end =====

# create folders and clean old files
mkdir -p ${ADX_DIR}
mkdir -p ${OBJDUMP_DIR}
mkdir -p ${LOG_DIR}
rm -f ${LOG_DIR}/*.log          # clean old logs
rm -f ${ADX_DIR}/*.adx          # clean old executables
rm -f ${OBJDUMP_DIR}/*.objdump  # clean old objdump files
#init global variable
pass_num=0
unsupport_num=0

ACTIVATION=(
    "activate_s8"
    "activate_s16"

    "relu_any_s16"

    "tanh_s8"
    "tanh_s16"

    "activate_s8_2buf"
    "activate_s16_2buf"
    "leaky_relu_s8_2buf"
    "relu_s8_2buf"
    "relu_s16_2buf"
    "relu_any_s8_2buf"

    "gelu_f16"
    "gelu_f32"
    "leaky_relu_f16"
    "relu_f16"
    "relu_any_f16"
    "sigmoid_f16"

    "fc_f16_f16_f16_bias"
)
POOLING=(
    "avepool_HWC_s8"

SOFTMAX=(
    "softmax_s8_fast"
    "softmax_s16_fast"
    "softmax_s8_hp"
    "softmax_s8_s16_hp"
    "softmax_u8_hp"
    "softmax_s16_hp"

    "softmax_f16"
    "softmax_f16_2pass"
    "softmax_f32"
    "softmax_f32_2pass"
    "softmax2d_f16"
    "softmax2d_f32"
)
UTIL=(
    "channel_shuffle_CHW_s8"
    "channel_shuffle_HWC_s8"
    "gather_HWC_s8"
    "gather_HWC_s16"
    "get_version_libnn"
    "lstm_unidirectional_s8"
    "lstm_unidirectional_s16_s8"
    "lstm_unidirectional_s16"
    "lut_linear_s16_513"
    "pixel_shuffle_HWC_s8"
    "reduce_sum_s8"
    "reduce_sum_s16"
    "requantize_s8_s8"
    "requantize_s16_s8"
    "requantize_s16_s16"
    "reshape_s8"
    "reverseV2_s8"
    "reverseV2_s16"
    "scatter_nd_s8"
    "scatter_nd_s16"
    "space_to_depth_HWC_s8"
    "svdf_s8"
    "svdf_s8_state_s8"
    "top_k_s8"
    "transpose_4d_s8"
    "transpose_4d_s16"
    "transpose_4d_s32"
    "upsampling2d_HWC_s8"
    "upsampling2d_HWC_s16"

    "argmax_f32"
    "decode_dist2box_f32"
    "decode_dist2box_rot_f32"
    "dequantize_s8_f16"
    "dequantize_s8_f32"
    "dequantize_s16_f32"
    "distributed_focal_loss_f32"
    "exp_f16"
    "exp_f32"
    "layer_norm_f16"
    "layer_norm_f32"
    "nms_f32"
    "quantize_f16_s8"
    "quantize_f32_s8"
    "quantize_f32_s16"
    "reshape_f16"
    "rms_norm_f16"
    "rms_norm_f32"
    "subspectral_norm_f32"
    "top_k_f16"
    "upsampling2d_HWC_f16"
)

NON_UNIT_STRIDE=(
    #ACTIVATION
    "activate_s8"
    "activate_s16"

    #FC
    "fc_s8_s8_s8_sym_bias_fast"
    "fc_s8_s16_s8_sym_bias_fast"
    "fc_u8_u8_s8_sym_bias_fast"
    "fc_u8_s8_s8_sym_bais_fast"
    "fc_u8_s16_s8_sym_bias_fast"
    "fc_s8_s8_s8_sym_fast"
    "fc_s8_s16_s8_sym_fast"
    "fc_u8_u8_s8_sym_fast"
    "fc_u8_s8_s8_sym_fast"
    "fc_u8_s16_s8_sym_fast"
    "fc_s8_s8_s8_sft_bias_fast"
    "fc_s16_s16_s16_sft_bias_fast"
    "fc_mat_vec_s16_s16_s8_sft_bias_fast"
)

if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${ACTIVATION[@]/%/:activation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_BASIC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${BASIC[@]/%/:basic}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONCATE_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONCATE[@]/%/:concatenation}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_CONV_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${CONV[@]/%/:convolution}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_FC_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${FC[@]/%/:fully_connected}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_POOLING_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${POOLING[@]/%/:pooling}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${SOFTMAX[@]/%/:softmax}")
fi
if [ ${ENA_TEST_ALL} == 1 ] || [ ${ENA_UTIL_TEST} == 1 ]; then
    test_list+=("${UTIL[@]/%/:util}")
fi

#remove non-unit-stride test cases
if [ ${ENA_UNIT_STRIDE_ONLY} == 1 ]; then
    for FUN_NAME in "${NON_UNIT_STRIDE[@]}";
    do
        test_list=(${test_list[@]/$FUN_NAME})
    done
fi

# record whether the zfh extension is enabled
if [[ ${CFLAGS} == *"-mzfh"* ]]; then
    ENA_FP16=1
else
    ENA_FP16=0
fi

# record whether the VL4 extension is enabled
if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ ENA_VL4 ]]; then
    ENA_VL4=1
else
    ENA_VL4=0
fi

if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    #init csv title
    echo "group, function, inst, cycle" > ${CSV_OUT}
fi

#----- enable the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="on"/CACHE="lm"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

for item in "${test_list[@]}"; do
    fun_name=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f1)
    fun_group=$(echo ${item} | cut -d ':' -f2)
    run_and_check "${fun_name}" "${fun_group}"
done

#----- restore the LM switch -----
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="lm"/CACHE="on"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi

# only show the execution summary as ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN is disabled
if [ ${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -eq 0 ]; then
    #show result summary
    total_num=${#test_list[@]}
    executed_num="$((total_num - unsupport_num))"
    printf "\n========== accuracy checking summary ==========\n"
    printf "Total functions:       %4d\n" "${#test_list[@]}"
    printf "Executed functions:    %4d\n" "${executed_num}"
    printf "Passed functions:      %4d (pass rate: %.2f%%)\n" "${pass_num}" "$(echo ${pass_num}*100/${executed_num} | bc -l)"
    printf "Unsupported functions: %4d\n" "${unsupport_num}"
fi

# show fail list
if [ ${#fail_list[@]} != 0 ]; then
    echo
    echo "failed funcion list:"
    for FUN_NAME in "${fail_list[@]}"; do
        echo ${FUN_NAME}
    done
fi

content.text: Now I have a good understanding of the full test infrastructure. Let me create the skill.

input.content:
---
name: verify-libnn
description: Build libnn and run functionality verification tests using Andes toolchain and QEMU
user_invocable: true
---

# libnn Functionality Verification Skill

You are verifying libnn functions for correctness using the Andes cross-compilation toolchain and Andes QEMU. Use this skill to build the library, run unit tests, and check accuracy results.

## Environment Setup

Every build/test command MUST be prefixed with these exports:

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/build-ast540/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
```

- **Andes Toolchain**: `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/riscv64-elf-gcc` (Andes v5d, GCC 14.2.0)
- **Andes QEMU**: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- The Andes toolchain MUST come first on PATH to override the system `riscv64-elf-gcc` (which lacks `-mext-vector` and other Andes extensions)

## Step 1: Determine What to Verify

Ask or determine from context:
1. **Scope**: Full test suite (all 294 functions) or a specific function/category?
2. **Target CPU**: Default is `AX45MPV`
3. **VLEN config**: Default is `v1024_d1024_b512` (VLEN=1024). Available configs for AX45MPV:
   - `v128_d128_b128`
   - `v256_d128_b128`
   - `v256_d256_b256`
   - `v512_d256_b256`
   - `v512_d512_b512`
   - `v1024_d512_b512`
   - `v1024_d1024_b512`

## Step 2: Build the Library

Build libnn for the target CPU:

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/build-ast540/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
./build.sh AX45MPV
```

This produces `libnn.a` and `libnn.objdump` in the repo root.

## Step 3: Run Tests

### Option A: Run Full Test Suite via run.sh

To run all 294 unit tests with a specific VLEN config, temporarily edit the QEMU wrapper selection in `project/ax45mpv/run.sh` (line ~96-103), then:

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/build-ast540/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
bash ./run.sh
```

**IMPORTANT**: Remember to revert `run.sh` after testing if you changed the wrapper.

The `run.sh` settings to be aware of:
- `RUN_CONFIG=2` — QEMU mode (default, keep as-is)
- `ENA_VEC_ISA=1` — Enable vector extension (default)
- `ENA_VDOT=1` — Enable vector dot product (default)
- `ENA_VL4=1` — Enable VL4 extension (default)
- `APP_RUN_CONFIG=2` — Function-level profiling with cycle counts (default)

### Option B: Run a Single Function Test

This is the preferred approach when verifying a specific function you've modified. Build and run the test executable directly:

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/build-ast540/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"

# 1. Build the library
cd /home/nick/work/libnn && ./build.sh AX45MPV

# 2. Compile the test
CC=riscv64-elf-gcc
CFLAGS="-Wall -Werror -O3 -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DPF_COUNTER -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS -mvh -DENA_VL4 -mzfh -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=0 -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0 -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
INC="-I/home/nick/work/libnn/Include -I/home/nick/work/libnn/libhvm-project/libhvm/include -I/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/unit_func/include"
LIB="/home/nick/work/libnn/libnn.a"

# Replace <func_name> with the test name, e.g., "softmax_f32", "relu_s8", "tanh_f32"
$CC $CFLAGS $INC -o /tmp/t_<func_name>.adx \
    /home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/unit_func/t_<func_name>.c $LIB -lm

# 3. Run via QEMU (VLEN=1024)
/home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 /tmp/t_<func_name>.adx
```

### Option C: Run a Category of Tests

Modify the flags in `Examples/unit_func/run.sh` to run only one category. Set `ENA_TEST_ALL=0` and enable the desired category:

- `ENA_ACTIVATION_TEST=1` — Activation functions (relu, sigmoid, tanh, silu, gelu, etc.)
- `ENA_BASIC_TEST=1` — Elementwise operations (add, mul, sub)
- `ENA_CONCATE_TEST=1` — Concatenation, pad, slice, split
- `ENA_CONV_TEST=1` — Convolution (1x1, HWC, depthwise, transposed, grouped)
- `ENA_FC_TEST=1` — Fully connected, batch matmul, vector sum
- `ENA_POOLING_TEST=1` — Average/max pooling
- `ENA_SOFTMAX_TEST=1` — Softmax variants
- `ENA_UTIL_TEST=1` — Utilities (NMS, layer_norm, exp, argmax, etc.)

## Step 4: Interpret Results

### Success Output
Each test prints:
```
The inst count is XXXXXX
The cycle count is XXXXXX
accuracy checking pass
```

Summary at the end:
```
========== accuracy checking summary ==========
Total functions:        294
Executed functions:     294
Passed functions:       294 (pass rate: 100.00%)
Unsupported functions:    0
```

### Failure Output
A failed test shows `accuracy checking FAIL !!!` or `fail` in its output. Failed functions are listed at the bottom.

### Performance Comparison
The `inst count` and `cycle count` values can be compared before/after optimization. Lower is better. Logs are saved to `Examples/unit_func/log/<func_name>.log`.

## Test Function Name Mapping

Test source files follow the pattern `Examples/unit_func/t_<name>.c`. The `<name>` maps to the library function:
- `t_relu_s8.c` tests `riscv_nn_relu_s8()`
- `t_softmax_f32.c` tests `riscv_nn_softmax_f32()`
- `t_conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any.c` tests `riscv_nn_conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any()`

Each test uses pre-computed golden data from `Examples/unit_func/bin/` for accuracy verification.

## Quick Reference: Common Verification Workflows

### After modifying a single function:
```bash
# Build + test one function (e.g., softmax_f32)
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/build-ast540/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
cd /home/nick/work/libnn && ./build.sh AX45MPV && \
riscv64-elf-gcc -Wall -Werror -O3 -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d \
  -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DPF_COUNTER -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS -mvh \
  -DENA_VL4 -mzfh -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=0 -DENA_RANDOM_INPUT=0 \
  -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP \
  -I./Include -I./libhvm-project/libhvm/include -I./Examples/unit_func/include \
  -o /tmp/t_softmax_f32.adx ./Examples/unit_func/t_softmax_f32.c ./libnn.a -lm && \
project/ax45mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 /tmp/t_softmax_f32.adx
```

### Full regression after multiple changes:
```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/build-ast540/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv && bash ./run.sh
```
Expect: 294/294 pass (100.00%)
